company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance - not a college-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.roadmap: Year 1 programming and Git; Year 2 DSA and core CS; Year 3 projects/internships and contests; Year 4 interview revision and applications.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance - not a college-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.roadmap: Build mathematics and Python first, then statistics/ML, projects, internships and case interviews.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance - not a college-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.roadmap: Year 1 circuits/programming; Year 2 digital/analog and HDL; Year 3 FPGA/VLSI projects and internship; Year 4 role-specific preparation.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance - not a college-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.roadmap: Master core subjects, use labs actively, complete one deep branch project, obtain industrial training and prepare GATE/core interviews.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance - not a college-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.roadmap: Build communication and analytics early, join case/entrepreneurship activities, seek business internships and practise cases/aptitude.

hostels_and_facilities.hostel_capacity: More than 5,000 students according to the 2025-26 brochure and May 2026 RTI disclosure
hostels_and_facilities.room_occupancy: Multiple occupancy is standard; exact hostel/year allocation varies
hostels_and_facilities.hostel_allotment_policy: Residential institute; current allocation is subject to availability and hostel-section rules
hostels_and_facilities.mess: Each hostel has a dining hall/mess. Quality is not officially rated.
hostels_and_facilities.canteen: Official campus and JoSAA profile list canteen facilities.
hostels_and_facilities.wifi: Officially Wi-Fi/LAN-enabled across hostels and campus; reliability is not officially quantified.
hostels_and_facilities.gym: Gymnasium hall with more than 15 exercise stations is described in the JoSAA profile.
hostels_and_facilities.sports: Badminton, tennis, volleyball, basketball, pool, table tennis, football and coaching camps are officially described.
hostels_and_facilities.library: Central Library and extensive print/e-resource inventory are officially listed.
hostels_and_facilities.laboratories: Departmental laboratories and Central Computer Centre are officially listed.
hostels_and_facilities.medical_centre: 24x7 health centre/OPD services with medical officers, visiting consultants, pharmacists and nursing staff.
hostels_and_facilities.ambulance: Government 108 ambulance is stated to be parked at the main gate; medical insurance covers ambulance charges subject to policy.
hostels_and_facilities.counselling_centre: Adolescent health counselling and student-welfare mechanisms are officially listed.
hostels_and_facilities.transport: Institute buses connect main gate, hostels and academic zones; powered vehicles by students are not permitted per JoSAA profile.
hostels_and_facilities.banking_atm: State Bank of India branch and ATM on campus.
hostels_and_facilities.shops: Post office and campus facilities are officially listed.
hostels_and_facilities.accessibility: Wheelchair-friendliness appears in a student report; detailed official accessibility audit not located.
hostels_and_facilities.security: Security department and main-gate control room are officially listed.
hostels_and_facilities.hostel_rules: Hostel brochure and section rules apply; exact 2026 first-year in-time requires manual confirmation.
hostels_and_facilities.anti_ragging: Institute and hostel-level anti-ragging squads and helpline are published.
hostels_and_facilities.grievance_mechanism: Hostel Management Committees, wardens, Dean Student Welfare, grievance committee and anti-ragging helpline are published.

location.college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology, Surat
location.official_address: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology, Ichchhanath, Surat - 395007, Gujarat, India
location.nearest_metro: No operational station verified in reviewed official sources
location.nearest_bus_terminal: Surat Central Bus Station / railway-station transport hub
location.nearest_major_hospital: New Civil Hospital Surat (map-based candidate; manually verify)
location.technology_ecosystem: Surat industrial and business ecosystem; Hazira industrial belt is accessible but not adjacent to campus
location.cost_of_living_band: Moderate for an Indian tier-2 city; map/student-derived and not an official institute estimate
location.data_origin: Address, station and airport distances official; coordinates and travel times map-derived approximations

senior_junior_culture.summary: Public sources conflict. One AMA says there was no ragging, while another second-hand review alleges uncomfortable senior introductions. Official anti-ragging squads and helplines exist, but the available evidence is not sufficient for a college-wide conclusion.
positive_themes: Some current-student comments report no ragging and approachable peers.
negative_themes: A limited source alleges uncomfortable senior-junior practices and language-based groups.

academic_culture.summary: Recent student accounts describe branch-specific faculty quality and substantial workload in some departments. CSE accounts highlight early core-subject exposure and freedom for self-directed work; EE comments report long days.
positive_themes: helpful faculty and research invitations in some departments
negative_themes: some branches report heavy schedules and limited spare energy

hostel_reality.summary: Official sources describe Wi-Fi/LAN, messes and common facilities, but student reports show large variation by building and year. Bhabha Bhavan and mess hygiene attract repeated recent complaints, while other accounts describe rooms and the girls mess more positively.
positive_themes: Some rooms are described as spacious
one AMA describes the girls hostel mess positively
hostel_reality.evidence_strength: Conflicting signal with moderate repeated negative signal for specific buildings

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official sources show a broad technical, cultural and professional chapter ecosystem, plus SPARSH and MINDBEND. Student accounts generally support an active event calendar, although club-selection fairness and first-year access are not well documented.
positive_themes: large green campus and recurring activities
negative_themes: limited independent evidence on selection fairness and actual activity level of every listed club

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: The clearest repeated theme is that coding culture is opportunity-rich but self-driven. Official recruiter and placement evidence supports access to major technology employers, while student comments stress that peers and personal initiative matter.
positive_themes: major technology and semiconductor recruiters are officially listed
negative_themes: coding culture is not uniformly strong across branches
non-CSE software-role eligibility is not transparently published

locality_and_student_life.summary: SVNIT is in an urban, relatively well-connected part of Surat, about 10 km from both railway station and airport. Students describe the surrounding area and campus walks positively, but evening freedom is affected by hostel in-time rules for some cohorts.
positive_themes: large campus and nearby developed areas
negative_themes: Surat may offer a smaller student/startup ecosystem than major metros
locality_and_student_life.evidence_strength: Moderate signal supported by official location facts

overall_satisfaction.summary: Overall satisfaction is materially mixed. Students value the NIT brand, urban location, established core departments, CSE opportunities and campus events. Concerns center on hostel variation, mess quality, workload, restrictions and uneven coding/sports culture.

parent_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
parent_mode_summary.summary: SVNIT Surat is a centrally funded Institute of National Importance with an urban Surat location, official 2026 seat matrix, tuition-remission rules and three years of official placement data. The main unresolved parent-facing risks are variable hostel conditions, incomplete 2026 mess/hostel cost, provisional 2025-26 placements and scholarship verification. Medical and ambulance systems are officially published.
priority_checks: Confirm allotted hostel and current charges
Use branch-specific rather than highest-package figures
Complete fee-remission and scholarship documentation early
Verify travel and emergency contacts during physical reporting

student_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
student_mode_summary.summary: SVNIT offers established engineering departments, newer AI/VLSI/MnC programmes, many technical and cultural chapters, and access to major technology/core recruiters. Student evidence suggests college life and coding opportunities can be good but self-driven, while hostel quality, workload and restrictions vary. Branch fit should take priority over college tag alone.
priority_checks: Ask current students from the same branch and hostel
Verify software-role eligibility for core branches
Join technical clubs and projects early
Build internship and interview preparation independently

decision_risks.analysis: Students seeking software roles from core branches should verify recruiter eligibility and plan self-driven coding; official branch-wise software eligibility is not published.
decision_risks.evidence_type: Official placement opacity plus student-reported signal

decision_risks.analysis: Official amenities do not establish quality. Recent reports indicate substantial variation by hostel building, especially Bhabha Bhavan.
Current-year rates may change and should not be used as final outcomes.
Potential eligibility does not guarantee sanction; current notices and document deadlines must be checked.
decision_risks.evidence_type: Official scheme listings with incomplete current details

decision_risks.analysis: Some recent students describe long schedules and early hostel in-time, which may conflict with students seeking extensive off-campus flexibility.

decision_risks.risk: New programmes have shorter historical evidence
decision_risks.analysis: AI, VLSI, Engineering Physics, Mathematics and Computing and integrated AI have less historical placement/cutoff evidence than established departments.
decision_risks.evidence_type: Calculated/inferred from programme and historical datasets

data_gaps: Official 2026 JoSAA/CSAB cutoffs are not yet available; 2023-2025 are historical evidence only.
Current programme-wise NBA accreditation and validity dates were not verified.
Current NAAC grade/validity and AICTE institute ID were not verified.
Final 2025-26 placement results are unavailable; the official summary is provisional through 31 May 2026.
Official current hostel allocation guarantee, exact room occupancy by year, laundry arrangements and 2026 first-year in-time were not confirmed.
Mess cost is not included in calculated programme cost because a verified 2026-27 annual mess amount for new entrants was not established.
Company-specific roles, branch eligibility, selected counts and packages are generally not published.
Official current scholarship amounts and deadlines for donor/government schemes require notice-level verification.
Nearest hospital, metro and technology-ecosystem distances require map/manual verification.
Student-experience evidence is limited, self-selected and materially conflicting.

conflicts.source_2_value: 2026 seat matrix uses Gujarat and Other Than Gujarat
conflicts.possible_reason: Historical export mapping or JoSAA archive representation changed for 2024.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Check the official 2024 JoSAA archive and map AI only after confirmation; do not silently convert.

conflicts.possible_reason: Different publication dates, classification or new/repurposed buildings. May 2026 RTI describes 10 hostels with another composition.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Confirm current 2026-27 operating hostel list directly with Hostel Section.

conflicts.possible_reason: Different eligibility, graduating-cohort and reporting definitions or reporting cut-off.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Preserve both; do not merge percentages without harmonizing scope.

conflicts.source_1_value: Official brochure lists standard facilities and administration
conflicts.source_2_value: Repeated building-specific maintenance, Wi-Fi and mess complaints
conflicts.possible_reason: Official pages describe availability, not quality; conditions vary by hostel and time.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Verify the specific hostel allocated to first-year students and inspect current mess/room conditions.

manual_verification_required: Confirm institute code 229 and main-campus identity before import.
Check the official 2024 JoSAA archive before mapping AI/All India to Other State.
Review the 31 blank-rank 2024 JoSAA rows and retain them outside numeric prediction inputs.
Keep CSAB All India CRL separate from JoSAA category-rank semantics.
Confirm all 15 programme names and the 1,400-seat 2026 matrix.
Confirm 2026-27 hostel inventory, allocation, occupancy, mess charges and in-time.
Confirm final 2025-26 placement statistics after the placement cycle closes.
Reconcile CDC 585 placed versus NIRF 581 for 2023-24 using definitions.
Verify current scholarship application notices, amounts and deadlines.
Verify club activity from dated 2025-26/2026 posts before marking active.
Treat all student allegations as student-reported and do not publish as official facts.
